title: ficus glomerata var. mollis
intro: Ficus glomerata var. mollis, also known as the cluster fig, is a variety of the ficus glomerata tree that is native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Ficus glomerata var. mollis have been found to promote overall health by reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into a tea or infused in oil to be used topically for its medicinal benefits.
